1._: Yarem pauses at the threshold to be seen before he enters, as is right among his people — a lean, weathered stranger in road-worn hides, his skin and his manner not of this country. He marks the carved counting-marks on the mantel, the maps weighted on the table, the keeper's measuring look — and knows the word for him in their eyes before they say it: barab, outlander. And on the draught through the door he catches the smell of the high cold he knows too well — the cold he had hoped to leave behind the mountains.

content._: yarem's account at Thousand Valleys, beat by beat.
